When bought: Construct :Engineer: 
Start of turn: :Attack: 
Click, pay :Attack: : Snipe :Engineer:
[2023-02-12 05:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): Thunderbolt 15 :Green: :Green: :Green: (4 supply)
Buildtime of 3 turns
Start of turn: 17 :Attack: , and sacrifice this unit.
[2023-02-12 05:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): Vivid Tarsier 7 :Red: :Drone: :Tarsier: :Tarsier: :Tarsier: (4 supply, 2 :Health: )
Start of turn: 5 :Attack:
[2023-02-12 05:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): Feedback is appreciated, but I have *reasons* to believe the balance is already pretty good on these.
[2023-02-12 05:45] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): I love more tarsiers please <3
[2023-02-12 05:47] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): Elemental spring seems...unnecessary? when are you going to have the economy to match that bonus tech, since you already need one of each to get that in the first place
[2023-02-12 05:48] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): Maybe sniping an engineer should cost more than one since one is already provided by the unit, or t he unit should not generate :Attack:
[2023-02-12 05:50] anesotericman (Esoteric): generating attack and swapping  is an elegant way to make the attack/defense prediction easier, I think
[2023-02-12 05:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): that's the fun part of the unit - it's a very efficient way to get tech, but it's balanced by the fact a lot of the tech is going to be redundant
[2023-02-12 05:51] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): It will just..never get used?
[2023-02-12 05:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): yeah, sniping units costing attack is there to help with defense calcs - it does snipe engis well and fuels itself, but it has the serious drawback it costs 9 - a lot more than a splitter
[2023-02-12 05:52] masn6811 (Masne98888): there are reasons to use it - for example, in a wincer build, it can replace the second forge. it does cost tempo when used that way, but you get green prod and red prod for "free", which can be worth it if you have any way to use it (cluster bolt, perf)
[2023-02-12 05:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): also, it's one of the best ways to get a third red, for units that want that, such as fang and ampo
[2023-02-12 05:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): it's not BSO viable, but there are a lot of advanced units that make it viable
[2023-02-12 05:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): here's another fun interaction - centrifuge!
[2023-02-12 05:54] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): I guess evyery unit has some synergy
[2023-02-12 05:54] silentslayers: I disagree, it's not that hard to find a case to use it
[2023-02-12 05:54] silentslayers: theres sinks for techs in the game
[2023-02-12 05:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): you can convert the burst of colored resources into a trickle, and you end up not overteching since you don't need to have all 3 tech buildings in the first place - this makes up for the slight inefficiency of fuge
[2023-02-12 05:54] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): I just don't have enough braincells to manage three resourced in a game tbh
[2023-02-12 05:54] silentslayers: high econ games would buy it
[2023-02-12 05:57] anesotericman (Esoteric): thunderbolt seem pretty miserable to play against in an auric set
[2023-02-12 05:57] masn6811 (Masne98888): i will say that although you expect it to be good in high econ, it's less good than it looks - that's a lot of tech, and you don't get too much control over the colors (eg, in a dgrid/odin set, you can use this to skip a forge, but it means you have to get an animus, which is not what you want). wincer has an overteching issue as well, since wincer is notorious for killing your econ to below your tech. still, it's worthwhile in centruion games, and especially worthwhile in fang games
[2023-02-12 05:58] masn6811 (Masne98888): i will also say that thunderbolt is not balanced for an environment where thunderbolt (or even doomed drone!) exists
[2023-02-12 05:58] masn6811 (Masne98888): it's likely not broken with doomed drone, since doomed drone also helps with the defense
[2023-02-12 05:58] masn6811 (Masne98888): the cost was chosen specifically so that natural conduit in DDC is one short, so p2 has to skip a drone to rush it
[2023-02-12 05:59] masn6811 (Masne98888): also, i should mention that where you do buy it, often the turn is this + DDD + float 6, which means it's a bit more expensive than it looks
[2023-02-12 06:00] silentslayers: yeah I know. red sink is pretty nice with it
[2023-02-12 06:00] silentslayers: my point was its mostly big econ that can use it, unless you get some tech cheat like centrifuge
[2023-02-12 06:02] masn6811 (Masne98888): i have a very large backlog of balanced and interesting units - i could do 1 per day for a long time if people are interested
[2023-02-12 06:04] anesotericman (Esoteric): Still wouldn't look forward to Thunderbolt mixed with anything like aurics. And any other large build-time stuff like wincer, endotherm combined with it would get unfun pretty quickly unless there's solid buildtime defense, even if the rushed version ends up balanced(?)
[2023-02-12 06:04] anesotericman (Esoteric): but yeah, always interesting to think about new units
[2023-02-12 06:08] masn6811 (Masne98888): wincer and endo therm aren't that big a concern actually
[2023-02-12 06:08] masn6811 (Masne98888): costing 15 gold, buying thunderbolt means a turn where you're likely floating all your tech
[2023-02-12 06:09] anesotericman (Esoteric): unless there's a sink, sure
[2023-02-12 06:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): the real problem is followups. for example, if it got followed up with a buildtime 2 unit that did 5 bursty attack, then 17 is barely managable early, but 22 is pretty scary
[2023-02-12 06:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): and then if there's nivos or clusters the turn after that, things quickly become unmanagable
[2023-02-12 06:10] masn6811 (Masne98888): time for the other player to spam gauss cannons i guess
[2023-02-12 06:10] masn6811 (Masne98888): (which isn't a terrible idea - thunderbolt is a huge investment and leaves your own drones fairly vulnerable to attack)
[2023-02-12 06:11] masn6811 (Masne98888): (and if the gauss cannons were to have 22 hp, then thunderbolt wouldn't even be able to kill them to stop the bleeding)
[2023-02-12 06:11] anesotericman (Esoteric): just seems like a less fun/interesting version of burst attack compared to other versions already in game to me (wincer, antima, endo-ish)
[2023-02-12 06:11] anesotericman (Esoteric): and wincer and antima are already controversial due to their sharpness
[2023-02-12 06:12] masn6811 (Masne98888): Leonardo "StrShPlyr" Vergara, when presented with the original design which did 18 attack (and was too strong), commented that it was a "worse wincer"
[2023-02-12 06:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): it's a much better design than antima imo - i've been playing a good amount of vanilla mata lately, and there are way too many games where one person just has the best antima timing and just wins
[2023-02-12 06:14] masn6811 (Masne98888): (i've undercut and lost, or let them have their timing and lost, depending on which seemed more likely to give me a chance)
[2023-02-12 06:14] anesotericman (Esoteric): yeah, likely less sharp than antima or wincer timings (though still sharp), but those two provide interesting situations that are otherwise uncommon.
[2023-02-12 06:15] anesotericman (Esoteric): things like what to do if opponent has 15 extra defense, or games you otherwise only get with savior/plasma where you are weighing out your last 5 drones versus killing 4 attack kind of stuff. They're more unique.
[2023-02-12 06:16] anesotericman (Esoteric): which has value for the first 100 games you play with them. Maybe less so after that

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content: theres sinks for techs in the game

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: you can convert the burst of colored resources into a trickle, and you end up not overteching since you don't need to have all 3 tech buildings in the first place - this makes up for the slight inefficiency of fuge

messages.author: vanilvanil
messages.content: I just don't have enough braincells to manage three resourced in a game tbh

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content: high econ games would buy it

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: thunderbolt seem pretty miserable to play against in an auric set

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: i will say that although you expect it to be good in high econ, it's less good than it looks - that's a lot of tech, and you don't get too much control over the colors (eg, in a dgrid/odin set, you can use this to skip a forge, but it means you have to get an animus, which is not what you want). wincer has an overteching issue as well, since wincer is notorious for killing your econ to below your tech. still, it's worthwhile in centruion games, and especially worthwhile in fang games

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: i will also say that thunderbolt is not balanced for an environment where thunderbolt (or even doomed drone!) exists

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: it's likely not broken with doomed drone, since doomed drone also helps with the defense

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: the cost was chosen specifically so that natural conduit in DDC is one short, so p2 has to skip a drone to rush it

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: also, i should mention that where you do buy it, often the turn is this + DDD + float 6, which means it's a bit more expensive than it looks

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content: yeah I know. red sink is pretty nice with it

messages.author: silentslayers
messages.content: my point was its mostly big econ that can use it, unless you get some tech cheat like centrifuge

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: i have a very large backlog of balanced and interesting units - i could do 1 per day for a long time if people are interested

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: Still wouldn't look forward to Thunderbolt mixed with anything like aurics. And any other large build-time stuff like wincer, endotherm combined with it would get unfun pretty quickly unless there's solid buildtime defense, even if the rushed version ends up balanced(?)

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: but yeah, always interesting to think about new units

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: wincer and endo therm aren't that big a concern actually

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: costing 15 gold, buying thunderbolt means a turn where you're likely floating all your tech

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: unless there's a sink, sure

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: the real problem is followups. for example, if it got followed up with a buildtime 2 unit that did 5 bursty attack, then 17 is barely managable early, but 22 is pretty scary

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: and then if there's nivos or clusters the turn after that, things quickly become unmanagable

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: time for the other player to spam gauss cannons i guess

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: (which isn't a terrible idea - thunderbolt is a huge investment and leaves your own drones fairly vulnerable to attack)

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: (and if the gauss cannons were to have 22 hp, then thunderbolt wouldn't even be able to kill them to stop the bleeding)

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: just seems like a less fun/interesting version of burst attack compared to other versions already in game to me (wincer, antima, endo-ish)

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: and wincer and antima are already controversial due to their sharpness

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: Leonardo "StrShPlyr" Vergara, when presented with the original design which did 18 attack (and was too strong), commented that it was a "worse wincer"

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: it's a much better design than antima imo - i've been playing a good amount of vanilla mata lately, and there are way too many games where one person just has the best antima timing and just wins

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: (i've undercut and lost, or let them have their timing and lost, depending on which seemed more likely to give me a chance)

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: yeah, likely less sharp than antima or wincer timings (though still sharp), but those two provide interesting situations that are otherwise uncommon.

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: things like what to do if opponent has 15 extra defense, or games you otherwise only get with savior/plasma where you are weighing out your last 5 drones versus killing 4 attack kind of stuff. They're more unique.

messages.author: anesotericman
messages.content: which has value for the first 100 games you play with them. Maybe less so after that

[2023-02-12 06:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): it's a FUCKING TERRIBLE! experience to roll an antime set when your opponent has a better timing - there's really nothing you can do - you can attack into the engi wall, or you can defend and have your opponent build up a huge stack of attackers because they have an engi wall

thunderbolt has a ton of counterplay - you can go breachproof, you can soak it (non prompt defense, and efficiency soak like polywall works wonders) - sure, there are broken combos with it, but a lot of units have broken combos and that's part of the fun of mata
[2023-02-12 06:20] anesotericman (Esoteric): yeah, I guess new units need to be at least slightly more juicy in some respect than all previous units or the game never really expands. Definitely would be fun to play with in sets with any counterplay.
[2023-02-12 06:20] anesotericman (Esoteric): and you're right that there are a bunch of units in prismata that are more broken than it if their set doesn't have counterplay
[2023-02-12 06:21] anesotericman (Esoteric): Maybe I just want a version of Prismata which always has at least one alternate econ unit and at least one buildtime blocker.
[2023-02-12 06:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): my design philophy is the game should be slightly unbalanced
[2023-02-12 06:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): player 2 has a slightly better conduit line, and player 1 has a slightly better blue line

[2023-02-12 21:43] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): could be interesting to have sauna/avanto units where they always come in a pair, but doing anything that changes set generation is a nonstarter for some people
[2023-02-12 21:44] masn6811 (Masne98888): they don't change set generation - these are two seperate units, and one can be present without the other
[2023-02-12 21:45] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): right, should have phrased that better. they reminded me that I hadn't explored 2-part/paired units as a design space
[2023-02-12 21:48] masn6811 (Masne98888): imo the recoil ray is an interesting red counter
[2023-02-12 21:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): although the rate doesn't look great on paper, a rule of thumb when playing green vs red is that extra damage on both sides is good for you
[2023-02-12 21:49] masn6811 (Masne98888): krypton ray is just a nice macro play - it's like a nerfed version of the original asteri cannon design
[2023-02-12 21:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): eh, instead of posting units im happy with, i should be posting units i'm not happy with
[2023-02-12 21:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): Monster (6 :Red: , supply 4)
When bought: Construct a :Wall: for your opponent.
Start of turn: :Attack: :Attack:
[2023-02-12 21:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): this feels to be on the weak side, when compared to rager, but i can't think of a good buff to it that doesn't make the rush broken
[2023-02-12 21:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): probably the spiciest idea is making it produce red - it's self balancing since if you use it to tech up to shadowfang, the wall will absorb it's own attack
[2023-02-12 21:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): it makes the perf interaction pretty spooky though
[2023-02-12 21:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): not really if it only makes a single red
[2023-02-12 21:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): then you need to buy two of those, and that significantly delays luci
[2023-02-12 21:58] masn6811 (Masne98888): that said, increasing cost to 6RR and income to XXR does feel interesting
